stories.body: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky removed Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi after nationwide protests and said popular Major General Mykhailo Drapatyi will lead a reorganization of the armed forces while former defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov, whose dismissal sparked the demonstrations, will return to government to help drive technological reforms and long-range drone strikes against Russia.
stories.headline: Zelensky fires army chief after protests push for front-line leadership change

stories.body: U.S. forces hit command centers, missile sites and air defenses in southern Iran for the 10th straight night in retaliation for American troop deaths, while Iran’s Revolutionary Guard claimed drone attacks on U.S. facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan and warned of tanker strikes as Iran-aligned Houthis threatened to blockade Saudi ports, prompting tankers loaded at Yanbu to reroute via the Suez Canal even as tentative mediation efforts seek a short cease-fire to rescue a collapsing interim peace deal.
stories.headline: Iran and U.S. trade fresh strikes as Houthis threaten Saudi shipping lanes

stories.body: France’s parliament approved President Emmanuel Macron’s plan to ban social media accounts for children under 15 starting September, with existing accounts to close by January 2027, obliging platforms to bolster age verification and dovetailing with a national mobile phone ban in high schools despite critics’ privacy concerns as the government argues the measure protects adolescents from harmful content.
stories.headline: France becomes first EU country to bar under-15s from social media

stories.body: Japan recorded its first kokushobi, or “cruelly hot day,” since the new category for temperatures above 40°C was created, with cities such as Gujo, Tajimi and Toyota setting all-time highs amid hundreds of stations exceeding 35°C, prompting advice for office workers to wear shorts, heightened concern over heatstroke deaths and accelerated shifts toward heat-tolerant crops and rescheduled sporting events.
stories.headline: Japan logs first official “cruelly hot” day as heatwave tops 40°C

stories.body: Election deniers Andy Biggs for governor, Alexander Kolodin for secretary of state and Warren Petersen for attorney general topped Arizona’s statewide Republican primaries while Trump-backed congressional hopefuls Mark Lamb and Jay Feely also won, underscoring the former president’s grip on the state party even as strategists warn the nominees’ 2020 rhetoric could alienate moderates in November.
stories.headline: Trump-aligned candidates sweep key Arizona GOP primaries

stories.body: The race to fill the late Senator Lindsey Graham’s seat has drawn multiple Republican contenders despite Donald Trump’s endorsement of appointee Darline Graham Nordone, with Representatives Ralph Norman and Russell Fry and businessman Mark Lynch all running, Trey Gowdy topping early polling despite professed disinterest, and party leaders debating eligibility rules, the risk of leaving House seats vacant and the limits of Trump’s clout after recent endorsement misfires.
stories.headline: South Carolina special election becomes latest test of Trump’s sway

stories.body: Tropical Storm Bertha stalled near the northern Gulf Coast with 40 mph winds, triggering tropical storm warnings from the Alabama-Florida border to southeastern Louisiana, storm surge watches near the Mississippi River delta, repeated sandbagging in New Orleans and forecast totals of 3 to 5 inches of rain with higher pockets plus possible tornadoes before the system drifts west toward Texas, while separate Hurricane Fausto spun harmlessly in the eastern Pacific.
stories.headline: Tropical Storm Bertha prompts evacuations and flood warnings along Gulf Coast

stories.body: Businessman Julio Martínez Martínez and Plus Ultra leaders told Spain’s National Court that former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero secretly steered consulting firm Análisis Relevante, arranged illegal commissions worth 1% of the airline’s 2021 state rescue and ordered payments to be dispersed through other companies, prompting prosecutors to seek further testimony as judge José Luis Calama examines alleged influence peddling networks linked to Venezuela, China and the Gulf.
stories.headline: Plus Ultra investigation deepens as executives tie Zapatero to bailout lobbying

stories.body: Lawyer Gonzalo Boye filed a complaint in Brussels on behalf of Carles Puigdemont and former ministers Toni Comín and Lluís Puig accusing Spain’s Court of Auditors of obstructing the new Catalan amnesty law by reopening allegations that EU funds financed the 2017 referendum, urging the Commission to launch infringement proceedings after the tribunal issued a press note that the defense says misrepresented last week’s European Court of Justice ruling.
stories.headline: Puigdemont asks European Commission to step in over Spain’s amnesty delays

stories.body: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Castilla-La Mancha’s president toured the La Mierla wildfire that has charred up to 32,000 hectares—one quarter of the Sierra Norte natural park and the largest blaze in regional history—forcing more than 1,300 evacuations, devastating riparian forests that shelter wolves and eagles, and mobilizing hundreds of firefighters and military personnel as Madrid prepares emergency aid covering half of the recovery costs.
stories.headline: Guadalajara megafire scorches 30,000 hectares as Sánchez visits disaster zone

stories.body: The disciplinary chief of Spain’s judicial council proposed shelving a probe into gender-violence judge David Maman—who oversees the sexual assault case against former national police chief José Ángel González—after concluding his comments about women having advantages when reporting partners, which drew a complaint from the Equality Ministry, were not sanctionable, though the council’s permanent commission postponed a final decision amid internal disagreement.
stories.headline: Judicial watchdog declines to punish gender-violence judge who said women have “advantages”

stories.body: Mayor Jaume Collboni wants Barcelona and the Catalan government to debate using public preemption rights to buy historic commercial spaces threatened by speculative rent hikes while the PSC prepares a council motion urging Spain to reform the Urban Leases Law to cap retail rents in overheated zones, a move ERC says copies proposals it has championed for three years amid closures of long-standing neighborhood businesses.
stories.headline: Barcelona studies fund to buy iconic shops as rent regulation push grows

stories.body: A brush fire that burned about 100 square meters near the closed antiaircraft batteries on Turó de la Rovira, allegedly sparked by fireworks from late-night drinking parties, has neighbors demanding tougher policing, alcohol seizures, more barriers and sanitation facilities after a string of assaults, property damage and traffic incidents, while the district promises summer portable toilets, reinforced fencing and stepped-up communications about site rules.
stories.headline: Fire at Turó de la Rovira renews calls to curb nightly parties at Barcelona overlook

stories.body: Night crews using a giant crane have begun installing 20-ton concrete slabs over a 340-meter segment of the Ronda de Dalt between the Vall d’Hebron institute and Vallcarca Avenue, laying 56 of 101 pieces by early August before outfitting the tunnel with lighting, ventilation and safety systems through 2027, part of Mayor Collboni’s pledge to extend the long-demanded covering to reconnect hillside neighborhoods and create 16,000 square meters of new public space.
stories.headline: Second stretch of Barcelona’s Ronda de Dalt ring road moves under cover

stories.body: Adif reported that the theft of signaling cable near Barcelona’s La Sagrera station caused high-speed trains between the city and Girona to run about 30 minutes late as maintenance teams worked to restore service, adding to ongoing frustrations with infrastructure reliability in Catalonia’s rail network.
stories.headline: Cable theft near La Sagrera causes high-speed rail delays between Barcelona and Girona
